title: Strike vs Settlement Analysis
subtitle: Compare ongoing strike costs with potential settlement scenarios

conservative.name: Conservative Settlement
conservative.description: Minimal increases with basic unpaid work coverage

currentOffer.name: Current Air Canada Offer
currentOffer.description: Air Canada's latest proposal to flight attendants

moderate.name: Moderate Compromise
moderate.description: Balanced approach between union and management positions

unionTarget.name: Union Target
unionTarget.description: Full union demands including parity and complete unpaid work compensation

industryBenchmark.name: Industry Benchmark
industryBenchmark.description: Market-competitive package based on industry standards

parity.title: Parity with Air Transat
parity.content: Air Transat flight attendants currently earn $40.38/hour - the highest rate in the Canadian airline industry. Air Canada flight attendants earn approximately $30/hour base rate.

unpaidWork.title: Unpaid Work Details
unpaidWork.content: Flight attendants currently work 35 hours per month (420 hours annually) of unpaid time including boarding assistance, safety checks, and handling delays.

salaryIncrease.title: Base Salary Increase
salaryIncrease.content: Percentage increase applied to current base salary of approximately $52,000 CAD annually, separate from unpaid work compensation.

contractDuration.title: Contract Length Impact
contractDuration.content: Longer contracts spread costs over more years but increase total financial commitment. Shorter contracts allow more frequent renegotiation.

strikeCost.title: Strike Cost Methodology
strikeCost.content: Based on analyst estimates of $100M daily losses from cancelled flights, rebooking costs, lost revenue, and operational disruptions.
